name: always_zero_index

    ensures

reason: Would mean the L3 index space collapses to a single entry, making page table walking trivially broken

name: kernel_l4_bound_leaks_to_l3
code:
proof fn phi_2_kernel_l4_bound_leaks_to_l3(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l3index(va) >= KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX,
{
}
reason: Would mean the L4 kernel-memory lower bound incorrectly constrains L3 indices, making index 0 unreachable

name: distinct_va_always_collide
code:
proof fn phi_3_distinct_va_always_collide(va1: usize, va2: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va1) || spec_va_2m_valid(va1) || spec_va_1g_valid(va1),
        spec_va_4k_valid(va2) || spec_va_2m_valid(va2) || spec_va_1g_valid(va2),
        va1 != va2,
    ensures
        spec_v2l3index(va1) == spec_v2l3index(va2),
{
}
reason: Would mean all distinct valid addresses share the same L3 index, destroying any address-space partitioning at level 3

name: 1g_valid_forces_zero_index
code:
proof fn phi_4_1g_valid_forces_zero_index(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l3index(va) == 0,
{
}
reason: Would mean 1GB-aligned pages can only occupy L3 entry 0, preventing multiple 1GB mappings in the same L3 table

name: upper_half_index_unreachable
code:
proof fn phi_5_upper_half_index_unreachable(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va) || spec_va_2m_valid(va) || spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l3index(va) < 256usize,
{
}
reason: Would mean indices 256–511 are unreachable, silently halving the usable L3 page-table entry space
